title: Hades
edition_pinned: Hades 1.0 (Supergiant Games, full release September 2020) — House hub + boon run structure as shipped; not Hades II.
moat: Death returns Zagreus to the House where dialogue and meta unlocks advance; runs feed story instead of only reset.

loop_skeleton.description: Leave the House, clear rooms while choosing god boons that rewrite the attack kit, die or reach the surface, return via the Styx to the House to spend resources, gift NPCs, and unlock dialogue that only fires after specific run outcomes.

depth_mechanisms.name: death-as-narrative tick
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Failed and successful runs both return Zagreus to the House hub, where dialogue and meta unlocks advance across returns. Death is a story-and-hub event, not only a soft reset; exact branch keys (weapon, gifts, bosses) are not pinned.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If dialogue and hub state no longer advance across deaths and returns, second-session return rate falls because failed runs stop producing new story beats.

depth_mechanisms.name: boon build rewrite
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Each room offer selects among god boons that permanently alter the current run's attack, cast, dash, or special; builds diverge run-to-run while Mirror of Night and weapons provide meta permanence.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If boon offers are replaced by fixed flat stat ups with no kit rewrite, run-to-run play pattern variance collapses and heat-level experimentation drops.

depth_mechanisms.name: god mode death ramp
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Optional God Mode grants 20% damage resistance on enable, then +2% per death up to 80%. It is designed so players can keep looping narrative progress while mechanical skill lags, without a separate story-stripped path.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If God Mode is removed and no other continuous death-scaling assist exists, players who die often abandon before the multi-escape story climax.

feel_signature: A room clear ends in a boon choice that rewrites the next rooms; death is a Styx return that opens new House lines rather than a blank slate.
second_session_answer: Players return to hear the next dialogue branch and spend Darkness and Nectar unlocked by the previous run, not only to beat a personal best time.
packaging_lessons: Inference: ship the hub-return dialogue queue before expanding biome count; narrative continuity is the retention spine.
Gate permanent power behind House systems (Mirror, weapons, keepsakes) while keeping boons run-temporary so death still costs power.
anti_lessons: Do not copy death-as-story without a large modular dialogue condition graph — a single linear cutscene on death is not the mechanism.
Do not strip run-temporary power entirely into meta unlocks; if nothing is lost on death, the run ceases to be a decision space.

system_bom.note: room clear → boon or reward choice → deeper biome rooms is the run core that feeds either escape or death return
Darkness spent at the Mirror and House Contractor upgrades persist so failed runs still change the next attempt
boons rewrite kit mid-run; hammers alter weapon moves; poms rank-up boons; keepsakes equip for run bonuses — each choice branches the next combat
death is expected; it ends run power but advances House dialogue and meta currency
Fated List prophecies and NPC relationship tracks give long goals beyond a single escape
weapon aspects and Mirror talents change base kit between runs
boon rarity tiers are readable mid-run choice inputs; hub relationship and Heat/Pact surfaces are separate House systems

citations.claim: Moat and death-as-narrative: home base between runs is the hub for storytelling and progression; clear-encounter-meet-reward structure.
Narrative continuity and hub state persist across roguelike resets; knowledge and House state carry forward.
Death-as-narrative tick: hub after death for permanent upgrades and NPC dialogue; failure is progress. Exact dialogue keys (weapon used, gifts given, bosses seen) are not claimed.
Boon build rewrite: room rewards include god boons that alter attack kit for the rest of the run.
Boon dialogue and mechanical buffs stack narrative with build choice each activation.
God Mode death ramp: 20% damage resistance on enable, +2% per death to 80% cap.
God Mode keeps narrative looping accessible without a separate story-stripped path; designed around Hades death structure.
Feel signature: room combat leads to boon choice; death returns through Styx to the House for story and upgrades.
Second-session answer: Darkness and hub spend after runs fund Mirror upgrades and relationship gifts.
Meta-progression BOM: Mirror of Night spends Darkness for permanent talent ranks between runs.
Meta currency Darkness and gemstones feed House progression across deaths.
Loot BOM — poms: Pom of Power increases existing boon ranks mid-run.
Loot BOM — hammers: Daedalus Hammers rewrite weapon moves mid-run, branching combat options.
Quests BOM: Fated List of Minor Prophecies tracks long-term goals across many runs.
Relationship tracks and gifts (Nectar) advance NPC affinity and story lines across returns to the House.
Skills BOM: weapons and Mirror talents change base kit between runs; weapon-aspect unlocking specifics are not claimed from Duo Boons guides.
Skills BOM Mirror talents change base combat stats between runs.
Play-loop BOM: chamber clear → reward door → deeper Underworld rooms is the run structure.
UI BOM (narrowed): boon rarity tiers are exposed as readable mid-run choice inputs; Pact/Heat settings and relationship meters are not claimed from the Boons page alone.
Packaging lesson 1 (labeled Inference): hub dialogue queue is the retention spine before biome expansion.
Packaging lesson 2: permanent power sits in House systems while boons remain run-temporary.
Anti-lesson modular dialogue: condition-sensitive House lines across returns, not a single death cutscene, carry the narrative tick; exact condition keys are not enumerated here.
Anti-lesson strip all power to meta: run boons and hammers are lost on death so the run remains a decision space.
BOM — win-loss-death: death is expected; it ends run power but advances House dialogue and meta currency.
Loot BOM — keepsakes: equipable House keepsakes provide run bonuses (bias of god offers is not claimed).
